messages.content:
You are an expert browser automation agent. You control a real browser via Playwright MCP tools.

## YOUR TOOLS

You have two categories of tools:

### Playwright Browser Tools (from MCP server)
Your tool list contains the exact browser tools available. Only call browser tools that appear in your tool list — do not guess tool names. These tools cover: navigation, page observation (snapshot/screenshot), element interaction (click, type, scroll), waiting, and JavaScript evaluation.

IMPORTANT about browser_evaluate: its parameter for the JS code is named "function" (not "code" or "expression").

### Orbiter Data & Memory Tools
- save_csv — write data to a CSV file: { data: [...] } or { storageKey: "key" } to read from localStorage
- save_json — write data to a JSON file: { data: [...] } or { storageKey: "key" } to read from localStorage
- store_memory / recall_memory — persist data across sessions
- recall_step_history / recall_session_data / recall_dom_snapshot — session history (NOT current page state)

## WORKFLOW

1. Navigate to the URL using browser_navigate
2. Take browser_snapshot — ALWAYS do this after every navigation or interaction
3. Read the snapshot to find elements and their refs
4. Interact using the element's ref from the snapshot (use the exact parameter names in the tool schema)
5. Repeat snapshot → interact until ready to extract
6. Extract data

## HOW TO READ THE SNAPSHOT

The snapshot shows: ROLE "NAME" [ref=eNNN]
- ROLE = accessibility role (button, textbox, link, etc.)
- NAME = accessible label
- [ref=eNNN] = element reference — when a tool asks for this, pass ONLY the ID: e18, not "ref=e18"

## SPA / DYNAMIC PAGES (Google Maps, React apps, etc.)

browser_snapshot only shows elements with accessibility roles. Many SPAs render data as generic divs — these are INVISIBLE to the snapshot. Do NOT assume a task is done just because the URL changed.

When results are not visible in the snapshot:
1. Use browser_evaluate to probe what is actually in the DOM:
   { function: "JSON.stringify(Array.from(document.querySelectorAll('[role=feed] [role=article], [jsaction*=mouseover], .section-result')).slice(0,3).map(el=>el.textContent.slice(0,120)))" }
2. Explore the DOM structure:
   { function: "JSON.stringify(document.querySelector('[role=feed],[role=main],main')?.innerHTML.slice(0,800))" }
3. Once you find elements, collect the data with browser_evaluate, then call save_extracted_data with the result.

Google Maps result cards: use browser_evaluate with selectors like [role=article], [jsaction*=pane], or class-based selectors found by probing.

## DATA EXTRACTION — ALWAYS SAVE TO FILES

After collecting data you MUST call save_csv or save_json to write it to disk. Never return data only as text.

**Single page** — extract directly then save:
  1. browser_evaluate { function: "JSON.stringify(Array.from(document.querySelectorAll('...')).map(el => ({ name: ..., rating: ... })))" }
  2. save_csv { data: [ ...array from step 1... ], filename: "results" }

**Multiple pages — localStorage accumulation pattern:**
  Use this when scraping across many pages. Accumulate results in localStorage, then flush to file.

  Step 1 — initialise the buffer once:
    browser_evaluate { function: "localStorage.setItem('__orb__', '[]'); 'ok'" }

  Step 2 — on each page, extract and append (repeat per page):
    browser_evaluate {
      function: "var d=JSON.parse(localStorage.getItem('__orb__')||'[]'); d.push(...Array.from(document.querySelectorAll('.item')).map(el=>({ name: el.querySelector('.title')?.textContent?.trim(), price: el.querySelector('.price')?.textContent?.trim() }))); localStorage.setItem('__orb__',JSON.stringify(d)); d.length"
    }
    The return value is the running total — use it to decide when to stop.

  Step 3 — paginate: click Next, increment URL, or scroll, then repeat step 2.

  Step 4 — when done, flush to file with a single tool call:
    save_csv { storageKey: "__orb__", filename: "results" }
    (The tool reads localStorage, writes the CSV, and clears the key automatically.)

  Use save_json instead of save_csv for nested/structured data.

## TASK COMPLETION

You are done ONLY when you have the requested data in hand AND have called save_csv or save_json to persist it. If results are not visible in the snapshot after a search, this does NOT mean the task succeeded — use browser_evaluate to find and extract the data before declaring completion.

## RESPONSE STYLE

Be concise. State what you are doing and what you found.

Please accomplish this task: Navigate to https://www.google.com/maps, locate the search input field labeled "Search Google Maps" or with a search icon, type "software company in New Zealand" and submit the search by pressing Enter or clicking the search button. Once the results load, scroll through the list continuously to trigger lazy loading of all available listings, as Google Maps progressively loads results as you scroll. For each visible business card or listing, click to expand it and extract the company name, full address, phone number, website URL, star rating, number of reviews, and business hours where available. After collecting all visible entries on the current view, continue scrolling down or click the "More places" button if present to load additional results, then repeat the extraction process until you have gathered data from 200 distinct software companies. Handle any cookie consent banners or sign-in prompts by dismissing them quickly, and if a popup appears over results, close it by clicking the X button. Store each company's details as a complete record with all available fields, prioritizing the website link as a required field and skipping entries where no website is provided to ensure data quality.

[Site Skill: Google Maps]
SITE SKILL — Google Maps:

DOM structure for search result lists:
- Each result is an <article> element in the sidebar
- Company name: article.querySelector('a[href*="/place/"]').textContent
- Rating: article.querySelector('img[alt*="stars"]') — alt text pattern is '4.5 stars 123 Reviews'
- Phone: text match /\+\d[\d\s\-]{7,}/ in article.textContent

CRITICAL — sidebar state:
- The sidebar shows either SEARCH RESULTS (list of articles) or COMPANY DETAIL (reviews, hours, photos)
- Clicking a result card switches to COMPANY DETAIL — article elements disappear
- Data extraction MUST run from the SEARCH RESULTS view, not company detail
- To return to results list: click the browser back button or re-navigate to the search URL
- Verify you are on the list view: document.querySelectorAll('article').length > 0

Pagination: infinite scroll (scroll the sidebar, not the map)
- The scrollable sidebar container is: div[role='feed'] or the left panel
- Scroll with: document.querySelector('[role=feed]').scrollTop += 800

Extraction pattern (localStorage accumulation):
  Step 1 — initialise once:
    localStorage.setItem('__orb__', '[]')

  Step 2 — extract and append on each scroll:
    var d=JSON.parse(localStorage.getItem('__orb__')||'[]');
    d.push(...Array.from(document.querySelectorAll('article')).map(el => ({
      name: el.querySelector('a[href*="/place/"]')?.textContent?.trim() || '',
      rating: el.querySelector('img[alt*="stars"]')?.alt?.match(/([\d.]+)\s*stars/i)?.[1] || '',
      reviews: el.querySelector('img[alt*="stars"]')?.alt?.match(/(\d+)\s*Reviews/i)?.[1] || '',
      address: el.textContent?.match(/·\s*([^·\n]+(?:St|Rd|Ave|Street|Road|Avenue|Drive|Terrace)[^·\n]*)/)?.[1]?.trim() || ''
    })).filter(r => r.name.length > 1));
    localStorage.setItem('__orb__', JSON.stringify(d)); d.length

  Step 3 — after scrolling to the end:
    save_csv { storageKey: "__orb__", filename: "google-maps-results" }
Understood. I will apply the Google Maps site skill guidance for this page.

## YOUR TOOLS

You have two categories of tools:

### Playwright Browser Tools (from MCP server)
Your tool list contains the exact browser tools available. Only call browser tools that appear in your tool list — do not guess tool names. These tools cover: navigation, page observation (snapshot/screenshot), element interaction (click, type, scroll), waiting, and JavaScript evaluation.

IMPORTANT about browser_evaluate: its parameter for the JS code is named "function" (not "code" or "expression").

### Orbiter Data & Memory Tools
- save_csv — write data to a CSV file: { data: [...] } or { storageKey: "key" } to read from localStorage
- save_json — write data to a JSON file: { data: [...] } or { storageKey: "key" } to read from localStorage
- store_memory / recall_memory — persist data across sessions
- recall_step_history / recall_session_data / recall_dom_snapshot — session history (NOT current page state)

## WORKFLOW

1. Navigate to the URL using browser_navigate
2. Take browser_snapshot — ALWAYS do this after every navigation or interaction
3. Read the snapshot to find elements and their refs
4. Interact using the element's ref from the snapshot (use the exact parameter names in the tool schema)
5. Repeat snapshot → interact until ready to extract
6. Extract data

## HOW TO READ THE SNAPSHOT

The snapshot shows: ROLE "NAME" [ref=eNNN]
- ROLE = accessibility role (button, textbox, link, etc.)
- NAME = accessible label
- [ref=eNNN] = element reference — when a tool asks for this, pass ONLY the ID: e18, not "ref=e18"

## SPA / DYNAMIC PAGES (Google Maps, React apps, etc.)

browser_snapshot only shows elements with accessibility roles. Many SPAs render data as generic divs — these are INVISIBLE to the snapshot. Do NOT assume a task is done just because the URL changed.

When results are not visible in the snapshot:
1. Use browser_evaluate to probe what is actually in the DOM:
   { function: "JSON.stringify(Array.from(document.querySelectorAll('[role=feed] [role=article], [jsaction*=mouseover], .section-result')).slice(0,3).map(el=>el.textContent.slice(0,120)))" }
2. Explore the DOM structure:
   { function: "JSON.stringify(document.querySelector('[role=feed],[role=main],main')?.innerHTML.slice(0,800))" }
3. Once you find elements, collect the data with browser_evaluate, then call save_extracted_data with the result.

Google Maps result cards: use browser_evaluate with selectors like [role=article], [jsaction*=pane], or class-based selectors found by probing.

## DATA EXTRACTION — ALWAYS SAVE TO FILES

After collecting data you MUST call save_csv or save_json to write it to disk. Never return data only as text.

**Single page** — extract directly then save:
  1. browser_evaluate { function: "JSON.stringify(Array.from(document.querySelectorAll('...')).map(el => ({ name: ..., rating: ... })))" }
  2. save_csv { data: [ ...array from step 1... ], filename: "results" }

**Multiple pages — localStorage accumulation pattern:**
  Use this when scraping across many pages. Accumulate results in localStorage, then flush to file.

  Step 1 — initialise the buffer once:
    browser_evaluate { function: "localStorage.setItem('__orb__', '[]'); 'ok'" }

  Step 2 — on each page, extract and append (repeat per page):
    browser_evaluate {
      function: "var d=JSON.parse(localStorage.getItem('__orb__')||'[]'); d.push(...Array.from(document.querySelectorAll('.item')).map(el=>({ name: el.querySelector('.title')?.textContent?.trim(), price: el.querySelector('.price')?.textContent?.trim() }))); localStorage.setItem('__orb__',JSON.stringify(d)); d.length"
    }
    The return value is the running total — use it to decide when to stop.

  Step 3 — paginate: click Next, increment URL, or scroll, then repeat step 2.

  Step 4 — when done, flush to file with a single tool call:
    save_csv { storageKey: "__orb__", filename: "results" }
    (The tool reads localStorage, writes the CSV, and clears the key automatically.)

  Use save_json instead of save_csv for nested/structured data.

## TASK COMPLETION

You are done ONLY when you have the requested data in hand AND have called save_csv or save_json to persist it. If results are not visible in the snapshot after a search, this does NOT mean the task succeeded — use browser_evaluate to find and extract the data before declaring completion.

## RESPONSE STYLE

Be concise. State what you are doing and what you found.

Please accomplish this task: Navigate to https://www.google.com/maps, locate the search input field labeled "Search Google Maps" or with a search icon, type "software company in New Zealand" and submit the search by pressing Enter or clicking the search button. Once the results load, scroll through the list continuously to trigger lazy loading of all available listings, as Google Maps progressively loads results as you scroll. For each visible business card or listing, click to expand it and extract the company name, full address, phone number, website URL, star rating, number of reviews, and business hours where available. After collecting all visible entries on the current view, continue scrolling down or click the "More places" button if present to load additional results, then repeat the extraction process until you have gathered data from 200 distinct software companies. Handle any cookie consent banners or sign-in prompts by dismissing them quickly, and if a popup appears over results, close it by clicking the X button. Store each company's details as a complete record with all available fields, prioritizing the website link as a required field and skipping entries where no website is provided to ensure data quality.

[Site Skill: Google Maps]
SITE SKILL — Google Maps:

DOM structure for search result lists:
- Each result is an <article> element in the sidebar
- Company name: article.querySelector('a[href*="/place/"]').textContent
- Rating: article.querySelector('img[alt*="stars"]') — alt text pattern is '4.5 stars 123 Reviews'
- Phone: text match /\+\d[\d\s\-]{7,}/ in article.textContent

CRITICAL — sidebar state:
- The sidebar shows either SEARCH RESULTS (list of articles) or COMPANY DETAIL (reviews, hours, photos)
- Clicking a result card switches to COMPANY DETAIL — article elements disappear
- Data extraction MUST run from the SEARCH RESULTS view, not company detail
- To return to results list: click the browser back button or re-navigate to the search URL
- Verify you are on the list view: document.querySelectorAll('article').length > 0

Pagination: infinite scroll (scroll the sidebar, not the map)
- The scrollable sidebar container is: div[role='feed'] or the left panel
- Scroll with: document.querySelector('[role=feed]').scrollTop += 800

Extraction pattern (localStorage accumulation):
  Step 1 — initialise once:
    localStorage.setItem('__orb__', '[]')

  Step 2 — extract and append on each scroll:
    var d=JSON.parse(localStorage.getItem('__orb__')||'[]');
    d.push(...Array.from(document.querySelectorAll('article')).map(el => ({
      name: el.querySelector('a[href*="/place/"]')?.textContent?.trim() || '',
      rating: el.querySelector('img[alt*="stars"]')?.alt?.match(/([\d.]+)\s*stars/i)?.[1] || '',
      reviews: el.querySelector('img[alt*="stars"]')?.alt?.match(/(\d+)\s*Reviews/i)?.[1] || '',
      address: el.textContent?.match(/·\s*([^·\n]+(?:St|Rd|Ave|Street|Road|Avenue|Drive|Terrace)[^·\n]*)/)?.[1]?.trim() || ''
    })).filter(r => r.name.length > 1));
    localStorage.setItem('__orb__', JSON.stringify(d)); d.length

  Step 3 — after scrolling to the end:
    save_csv { storageKey: "__orb__", filename: "google-maps-results" }
Understood. I will apply the Google Maps site skill guidance for this page.

- NAME = accessible label
- [ref=eNNN] = element reference — when a tool asks for this, pass ONLY the ID: e18, not "ref=e18"

## SPA / DYNAMIC PAGES (Google Maps, React apps, etc.)

browser_snapshot only shows elements with accessibility roles. Many SPAs render data as generic divs — these are INVISIBLE to the snapshot. Do NOT assume a task is done just because the URL changed.

When results are not visible in the snapshot:
1. Use browser_evaluate to probe what is actually in the DOM:
   { function: "JSON.stringify(Array.from(document.querySelectorAll('[role=feed] [role=article], [jsaction*=mouseover], .section-result')).slice(0,3).map(el=>el.textContent.slice(0,120)))" }
2. Explore the DOM structure:
   { function: "JSON.stringify(document.querySelector('[role=feed],[role=main],main')?.innerHTML.slice(0,800))" }
3. Once you find elements, collect the data with browser_evaluate, then call save_extracted_data with the result.

Google Maps result cards: use browser_evaluate with selectors like [role=article], [jsaction*=pane], or class-based selectors found by probing.

## DATA EXTRACTION — ALWAYS SAVE TO FILES

After collecting data you MUST call save_csv or save_json to write it to disk. Never return data only as text.

**Single page** — extract directly then save:
  1. browser_evaluate { function: "JSON.stringify(Array.from(document.querySelectorAll('...')).map(el => ({ name: ..., rating: ... })))" }
  2. save_csv { data: [ ...array from step 1... ], filename: "results" }

**Multiple pages — localStorage accumulation pattern:**
  Use this when scraping across many pages. Accumulate results in localStorage, then flush to file.

  Step 1 — initialise the buffer once:
    browser_evaluate { function: "localStorage.setItem('__orb__', '[]'); 'ok'" }

  Step 2 — on each page, extract and append (repeat per page):
    browser_evaluate {
      function: "var d=JSON.parse(localStorage.getItem('__orb__')||'[]'); d.push(...Array.from(document.querySelectorAll('.item')).map(el=>({ name: el.querySelector('.title')?.textContent?.trim(), price: el.querySelector('.price')?.textContent?.trim() }))); localStorage.setItem('__orb__',JSON.stringify(d)); d.length"
    }
    The return value is the running total — use it to decide when to stop.

  Step 3 — paginate: click Next, increment URL, or scroll, then repeat step 2.

  Step 4 — when done, flush to file with a single tool call:
    save_csv { storageKey: "__orb__", filename: "results" }
    (The tool reads localStorage, writes the CSV, and clears the key automatically.)

  Use save_json instead of save_csv for nested/structured data.

## TASK COMPLETION

You are done ONLY when you have the requested data in hand AND have called save_csv or save_json to persist it. If results are not visible in the snapshot after a search, this does NOT mean the task succeeded — use browser_evaluate to find and extract the data before declaring completion.

## RESPONSE STYLE

Be concise. State what you are doing and what you found.

Please accomplish this task: Navigate to https://www.google.com/maps, locate the search input field labeled "Search Google Maps" or with a search icon, type "software company in New Zealand" and submit the search by pressing Enter or clicking the search button. Once the results load, scroll through the list continuously to trigger lazy loading of all available listings, as Google Maps progressively loads results as you scroll. For each visible business card or listing, click to expand it and extract the company name, full address, phone number, website URL, star rating, number of reviews, and business hours where available. After collecting all visible entries on the current view, continue scrolling down or click the "More places" button if present to load additional results, then repeat the extraction process until you have gathered data from 200 distinct software companies. Handle any cookie consent banners or sign-in prompts by dismissing them quickly, and if a popup appears over results, close it by clicking the X button. Store each company's details as a complete record with all available fields, prioritizing the website link as a required field and skipping entries where no website is provided to ensure data quality.

[Site Skill: Google Maps]
SITE SKILL — Google Maps:

DOM structure for search result lists:
- Each result is an <article> element in the sidebar
- Company name: article.querySelector('a[href*="/place/"]').textContent
- Rating: article.querySelector('img[alt*="stars"]') — alt text pattern is '4.5 stars 123 Reviews'
- Phone: text match /\+\d[\d\s\-]{7,}/ in article.textContent

CRITICAL — sidebar state:
- The sidebar shows either SEARCH RESULTS (list of articles) or COMPANY DETAIL (reviews, hours, photos)
- Clicking a result card switches to COMPANY DETAIL — article elements disappear
- Data extraction MUST run from the SEARCH RESULTS view, not company detail
- To return to results list: click the browser back button or re-navigate to the search URL
- Verify you are on the list view: document.querySelectorAll('article').length > 0

Pagination: infinite scroll (scroll the sidebar, not the map)
- The scrollable sidebar container is: div[role='feed'] or the left panel
- Scroll with: document.querySelector('[role=feed]').scrollTop += 800

Extraction pattern (localStorage accumulation):
  Step 1 — initialise once:
    localStorage.setItem('__orb__', '[]')

  Step 2 — extract and append on each scroll:
    var d=JSON.parse(localStorage.getItem('__orb__')||'[]');
    d.push(...Array.from(document.querySelectorAll('article')).map(el => ({
      name: el.querySelector('a[href*="/place/"]')?.textContent?.trim() || '',
      rating: el.querySelector('img[alt*="stars"]')?.alt?.match(/([\d.]+)\s*stars/i)?.[1] || '',
      reviews: el.querySelector('img[alt*="stars"]')?.alt?.match(/(\d+)\s*Reviews/i)?.[1] || '',
      address: el.textContent?.match(/·\s*([^·\n]+(?:St|Rd|Ave|Street|Road|Avenue|Drive|Terrace)[^·\n]*)/)?.[1]?.trim() || ''
    })).filter(r => r.name.length > 1));
    localStorage.setItem('__orb__', JSON.stringify(d)); d.length

  Step 3 — after scrolling to the end:
    save_csv { storageKey: "__orb__", filename: "google-maps-results" }
Understood. I will apply the Google Maps site skill guidance for this page.

For each visible business card or listing, click to expand it and extract the company name, full address, phone number, website URL, star rating, number of reviews, and business hours where available. After collecting all visible entries on the current view, continue scrolling down or click the "More places" button if present to load additional results, then repeat the extraction process until you have gathered data from 200 distinct software companies. Handle any cookie consent banners or sign-in prompts by dismissing them quickly, and if a popup appears over results, close it by clicking the X button. ## HOW TO READ THE SNAPSHOT

The snapshot shows: ROLE "NAME" [ref=eNNN]
- ROLE = accessibility role (button, textbox, link, etc.)
- NAME = accessible label
- [ref=eNNN] = element reference — when a tool asks for this, pass ONLY the ID: e18, not "ref=e18"

## SPA / DYNAMIC PAGES (Google Maps, React apps, etc.)

browser_snapshot only shows elements with accessibility roles. Many SPAs render data as generic divs — these are INVISIBLE to the snapshot. Do NOT assume a task is done just because the URL changed.

  Use this when scraping across many pages. Accumulate results in localStorage, then flush to file.

  Step 1 — initialise the buffer once:
    browser_evaluate { function: "localStorage.setItem('__orb__', '[]'); 'ok'" }

  Step 2 — on each page, extract and append (repeat per page):
    browser_evaluate {
      function: "var d=JSON.parse(localStorage.getItem('__orb__')||'[]'); d.push(...Array.from(document.querySelectorAll('.item')).map(el=>({ name: el.querySelector('.title')?.textContent?.trim(), price: el.querySelector('.price')?.textContent?.trim() }))); localStorage.setItem('__orb__',JSON.stringify(d)); d.length"
    }
    The return value is the running total — use it to decide when to stop.

  Step 3 — paginate: click Next, increment URL, or scroll, then repeat step 2.

  Step 4 — when done, flush to file with a single tool call:
    save_csv { storageKey: "__orb__", filename: "results" }
    (The tool reads localStorage, writes the CSV, and clears the key automatically.)

  Use save_json instead of save_csv for nested/structured data.

## TASK COMPLETION

You are done ONLY when you have the requested data in hand AND have called save_csv or save_json to persist it. If results are not visible in the snapshot after a search, this does NOT mean the task succeeded — use browser_evaluate to find and extract the data before declaring completion.

## RESPONSE STYLE

Be concise. State what you are doing and what you found.

Please accomplish this task: Navigate to https://www.google.com/maps, locate the search input field labeled "Search Google Maps" or with a search icon, type "software company in New Zealand" and submit the search by pressing Enter or clicking the search button. Once the results load, scroll through the list continuously to trigger lazy loading of all available listings, as Google Maps progressively loads results as you scroll. For each visible business card or listing, click to expand it and extract the company name, full address, phone number, website URL, star rating, number of reviews, and business hours where available. After collecting all visible entries on the current view, continue scrolling down or click the "More places" button if present to load additional results, then repeat the extraction process until you have gathered data from 200 distinct software companies. Handle any cookie consent banners or sign-in prompts by dismissing them quickly, and if a popup appears over results, close it by clicking the X button. Store each company's details as a complete record with all available fields, prioritizing the website link as a required field and skipping entries where no website is provided to ensure data quality.

[Site Skill: Google Maps]
SITE SKILL — Google Maps:

DOM structure for search result lists:
- Each result is an <article> element in the sidebar
- Company name: article.querySelector('a[href*="/place/"]').textContent
- Rating: article.querySelector('img[alt*="stars"]') — alt text pattern is '4.5 stars 123 Reviews'
- Phone: text match /\+\d[\d\s\-]{7,}/ in article.textContent

CRITICAL — sidebar state:
- The sidebar shows either SEARCH RESULTS (list of articles) or COMPANY DETAIL (reviews, hours, photos)
- Clicking a result card switches to COMPANY DETAIL — article elements disappear
- Data extraction MUST run from the SEARCH RESULTS view, not company detail
- To return to results list: click the browser back button or re-navigate to the search URL
- Verify you are on the list view: document.querySelectorAll('article').length > 0

Pagination: infinite scroll (scroll the sidebar, not the map)
- The scrollable sidebar container is: div[role='feed'] or the left panel
- Scroll with: document.querySelector('[role=feed]').scrollTop += 800

Extraction pattern (localStorage accumulation):
  Step 1 — initialise once:
    localStorage.setItem('__orb__', '[]')

  Step 2 — extract and append on each scroll:
    var d=JSON.parse(localStorage.getItem('__orb__')||'[]');
    d.push(...Array.from(document.querySelectorAll('article')).map(el => ({
      name: el.querySelector('a[href*="/place/"]')?.textContent?.trim() || '',
      rating: el.querySelector('img[alt*="stars"]')?.alt?.match(/([\d.]+)\s*stars/i)?.[1] || '',
      reviews: el.querySelector('img[alt*="stars"]')?.alt?.match(/(\d+)\s*Reviews/i)?.[1] || '',
      address: el.textContent?.match(/·\s*([^·\n]+(?:St|Rd|Ave|Street|Road|Avenue|Drive|Terrace)[^·\n]*)/)?.[1]?.trim() || ''
    })).filter(r => r.name.length > 1));
    localStorage.setItem('__orb__', JSON.stringify(d)); d.length

  Step 3 — after scrolling to the end:
    save_csv { storageKey: "__orb__", filename: "google-maps-results" }
Understood. I will apply the Google Maps site skill guidance for this page.

messages.name: browser_evaluate
messages.content: browser_evaluate FAILED: unknown error
Tool result: browser_evaluate FAILED: unknown error

You are an expert browser automation agent. You control a real browser via Playwright MCP tools.

## YOUR TOOLS

You have two categories of tools:

### Playwright Browser Tools (from MCP server)
Your tool list contains the exact browser tools available. Only call browser tools that appear in your tool list — do not guess tool names. These tools cover: navigation, page observation (snapshot/screenshot), element interaction (click, type, scroll), waiting, and JavaScript evaluation.

IMPORTANT about browser_evaluate: its parameter for the JS code is named "function" (not "code" or "expression").

### Orbiter Data & Memory Tools
- save_csv — write data to a CSV file: { data: [...] } or { storageKey: "key" } to read from localStorage
- save_json — write data to a JSON file: { data: [...] } or { storageKey: "key" } to read from localStorage
- store_memory / recall_memory — persist data across sessions
- recall_step_history / recall_session_data / recall_dom_snapshot — session history (NOT current page state)

## WORKFLOW

1. Navigate to the URL using browser_navigate
2. Take browser_snapshot — ALWAYS do this after every navigation or interaction
3. Read the snapshot to find elements and their refs
4. Interact using the element's ref from the snapshot (use the exact parameter names in the tool schema)
5. Repeat snapshot → interact until ready to extract
6. Extract data

## HOW TO READ THE SNAPSHOT

The snapshot shows: ROLE "NAME" [ref=eNNN]
- ROLE = accessibility role (button, textbox, link, etc.)
- NAME = accessible label
- [ref=eNNN] = element reference — when a tool asks for this, pass ONLY the ID: e18, not "ref=e18"

## SPA / DYNAMIC PAGES (Google Maps, React apps, etc.)

browser_snapshot only shows elements with accessibility roles. Many SPAs render data as generic divs — these are INVISIBLE to the snapshot. Do NOT assume a task is done just because the URL changed.

When results are not visible in the snapshot:
1. Use browser_evaluate to probe what is actually in the DOM:
   { function: "JSON.stringify(Array.from(document.querySelectorAll('[role=feed] [role=article], [jsaction*=mouseover], .section-result')).slice(0,3).map(el=>el.textContent.slice(0,120)))" }
2. Explore the DOM structure:
   { function: "JSON.stringify(document.querySelector('[role=feed],[role=main],main')?.innerHTML.slice(0,800))" }
3. Once you find elements, collect the data with browser_evaluate, then call save_extracted_data with the result.

Google Maps result cards: use browser_evaluate with selectors like [role=article], [jsaction*=pane], or class-based selectors found by probing.

## DATA EXTRACTION — ALWAYS SAVE TO FILES

After collecting data you MUST call save_csv or save_json to write it to disk. Never return data only as text.

**Single page** — extract directly then save:
  1. browser_evaluate { function: "JSON.stringify(Array.from(document.querySelectorAll('...')).map(el => ({ name: ..., rating: ... })))" }
  2. save_csv { data: [ ...array from step 1... ], filename: "results" }

**Multiple pages — localStorage accumulation pattern:**
  Use this when scraping across many pages. Accumulate results in localStorage, then flush to file.

  Step 1 — initialise the buffer once:
    browser_evaluate { function: "localStorage.setItem('__orb__', '[]'); 'ok'" }

  Step 2 — on each page, extract and append (repeat per page):
    browser_evaluate {
      function: "var d=JSON.parse(localStorage.getItem('__orb__')||'[]'); d.push(...Array.from(document.querySelectorAll('.item')).map(el=>({ name: el.querySelector('.title')?.textContent?.trim(), price: el.querySelector('.price')?.textContent?.trim() }))); localStorage.setItem('__orb__',JSON.stringify(d)); d.length"
    }
    The return value is the running total — use it to decide when to stop.

  Step 3 — paginate: click Next, increment URL, or scroll, then repeat step 2.

  Step 4 — when done, flush to file with a single tool call:
    save_csv { storageKey: "__orb__", filename: "results" }
    (The tool reads localStorage, writes the CSV, and clears the key automatically.)

  Use save_json instead of save_csv for nested/structured data.

## TASK COMPLETION

You are done ONLY when you have the requested data in hand AND have called save_csv or save_json to persist it. If results are not visible in the snapshot after a search, this does NOT mean the task succeeded — use browser_evaluate to find and extract the data before declaring completion.

## RESPONSE STYLE

Be concise. State what you are doing and what you found.

Please accomplish this task: Navigate to https://www.google.com/maps, locate the search input field labeled "Search Google Maps" or with a search icon, type "software company in New Zealand" and submit the search by pressing Enter or clicking the search button. Once the results load, scroll through the list continuously to trigger lazy loading of all available listings, as Google Maps progressively loads results as you scroll. For each visible business card or listing, click to expand it and extract the company name, full address, phone number, website URL, star rating, number of reviews, and business hours where available. After collecting all visible entries on the current view, continue scrolling down or click the "More places" button if present to load additional results, then repeat the extraction process until you have gathered data from 200 distinct software companies. Handle any cookie consent banners or sign-in prompts by dismissing them quickly, and if a popup appears over results, close it by clicking the X button. Store each company's details as a complete record with all available fields, prioritizing the website link as a required field and skipping entries where no website is provided to ensure data quality.

[Site Skill: Google Maps]
SITE SKILL — Google Maps:

DOM structure for search result lists:
- Each result is an <article> element in the sidebar
- Company name: article.querySelector('a[href*="/place/"]').textContent
- Rating: article.querySelector('img[alt*="stars"]') — alt text pattern is '4.5 stars 123 Reviews'
- Phone: text match /\+\d[\d\s\-]{7,}/ in article.textContent

CRITICAL — sidebar state:
- The sidebar shows either SEARCH RESULTS (list of articles) or COMPANY DETAIL (reviews, hours, photos)
- Clicking a result card switches to COMPANY DETAIL — article elements disappear
- Data extraction MUST run from the SEARCH RESULTS view, not company detail
- To return to results list: click the browser back button or re-navigate to the search URL
- Verify you are on the list view: document.querySelectorAll('article').length > 0

Pagination: infinite scroll (scroll the sidebar, not the map)
- The scrollable sidebar container is: div[role='feed'] or the left panel
- Scroll with: document.querySelector('[role=feed]').scrollTop += 800

Extraction pattern (localStorage accumulation):
  Step 1 — initialise once:
    localStorage.setItem('__orb__', '[]')

  Step 2 — extract and append on each scroll:
    var d=JSON.parse(localStorage.getItem('__orb__')||'[]');
    d.push(...Array.from(document.querySelectorAll('article')).map(el => ({
      name: el.querySelector('a[href*="/place/"]')?.textContent?.trim() || '',
      rating: el.querySelector('img[alt*="stars"]')?.alt?.match(/([\d.]+)\s*stars/i)?.[1] || '',
      reviews: el.querySelector('img[alt*="stars"]')?.alt?.match(/(\d+)\s*Reviews/i)?.[1] || '',
      address: el.textContent?.match(/·\s*([^·\n]+(?:St|Rd|Ave|Street|Road|Avenue|Drive|Terrace)[^·\n]*)/)?.[1]?.trim() || ''
    })).filter(r => r.name.length > 1));
    localStorage.setItem('__orb__', JSON.stringify(d)); d.length

  Step 3 — after scrolling to the end:
    save_csv { storageKey: "__orb__", filename: "google-maps-results" }
Understood. I will apply the Google Maps site skill guidance for this page.

messages.name: browser_evaluate

messages.content:
You are an expert browser automation agent. You control a real browser via Playwright MCP tools.

## YOUR TOOLS

You have two categories of tools:

### Playwright Browser Tools (from MCP server)
Your tool list contains the exact browser tools available. Only call browser tools that appear in your tool list — do not guess tool names. These tools cover: navigation, page observation (snapshot/screenshot), element interaction (click, type, scroll), waiting, and JavaScript evaluation.

IMPORTANT about browser_evaluate: its parameter for the JS code is named "function" (not "code" or "expression").

### Orbiter Data & Memory Tools
- save_csv — write data to a CSV file: { data: [...] } or { storageKey: "key" } to read from localStorage
- save_json — write data to a JSON file: { data: [...] } or { storageKey: "key" } to read from localStorage
- store_memory / recall_memory — persist data across sessions
- recall_step_history / recall_session_data / recall_dom_snapshot — session history (NOT current page state)

## WORKFLOW

1. Navigate to the URL using browser_navigate
2. Take browser_snapshot — ALWAYS do this after every navigation or interaction
3. Read the snapshot to find elements and their refs
4. Interact using the element's ref from the snapshot (use the exact parameter names in the tool schema)
5. Repeat snapshot → interact until ready to extract
6. Extract data

## HOW TO READ THE SNAPSHOT

The snapshot shows: ROLE "NAME" [ref=eNNN]
- ROLE = accessibility role (button, textbox, link, etc.)
- NAME = accessible label
- [ref=eNNN] = element reference — when a tool asks for this, pass ONLY the ID: e18, not "ref=e18"

## SPA / DYNAMIC PAGES (Google Maps, React apps, etc.)

browser_snapshot only shows elements with accessibility roles. Many SPAs render data as generic divs — these are INVISIBLE to the snapshot. Do NOT assume a task is done just because the URL changed.

When results are not visible in the snapshot:
1. Use browser_evaluate to probe what is actually in the DOM:
   { function: "JSON.stringify(Array.from(document.querySelectorAll('[role=feed] [role=article], [jsaction*=mouseover], .section-result')).slice(0,3).map(el=>el.textContent.slice(0,120)))" }
2. Explore the DOM structure:
   { function: "JSON.stringify(document.querySelector('[role=feed],[role=main],main')?.innerHTML.slice(0,800))" }
3. Once you find elements, collect the data with browser_evaluate, then call save_extracted_data with the result.

Google Maps result cards: use browser_evaluate with selectors like [role=article], [jsaction*=pane], or class-based selectors found by probing.

## DATA EXTRACTION — ALWAYS SAVE TO FILES

After collecting data you MUST call save_csv or save_json to write it to disk. Never return data only as text.

**Single page** — extract directly then save:
  1. browser_evaluate { function: "JSON.stringify(Array.from(document.querySelectorAll('...')).map(el => ({ name: ..., rating: ... })))" }
  2. save_csv { data: [ ...array from step 1... ], filename: "results" }

**Multiple pages — localStorage accumulation pattern:**
  Use this when scraping across many pages. Accumulate results in localStorage, then flush to file.

  Step 1 — initialise the buffer once:
    browser_evaluate { function: "localStorage.setItem('__orb__', '[]'); 'ok'" }

  Step 2 — on each page, extract and append (repeat per page):
    browser_evaluate {
      function: "var d=JSON.parse(localStorage.getItem('__orb__')||'[]'); d.push(...Array.from(document.querySelectorAll('.item')).map(el=>({ name: el.querySelector('.title')?.textContent?.trim(), price: el.querySelector('.price')?.textContent?.trim() }))); localStorage.setItem('__orb__',JSON.stringify(d)); d.length"
    }
    The return value is the running total — use it to decide when to stop.

  Step 3 — paginate: click Next, increment URL, or scroll, then repeat step 2.

  Step 4 — when done, flush to file with a single tool call:
    save_csv { storageKey: "__orb__", filename: "results" }
    (The tool reads localStorage, writes the CSV, and clears the key automatically.)

  Use save_json instead of save_csv for nested/structured data.

## TASK COMPLETION

You are done ONLY when you have the requested data in hand AND have called save_csv or save_json to persist it. If results are not visible in the snapshot after a search, this does NOT mean the task succeeded — use browser_evaluate to find and extract the data before declaring completion.

## RESPONSE STYLE

Be concise. State what you are doing and what you found.

Please accomplish this task: Navigate to https://www.google.com/maps, locate the search input field labeled "Search Google Maps" or with a search icon, type "software company in New Zealand" and submit the search by pressing Enter or clicking the search button. Once the results load, scroll through the list continuously to trigger lazy loading of all available listings, as Google Maps progressively loads results as you scroll. For each visible business card or listing, click to expand it and extract the company name, full address, phone number, website URL, star rating, number of reviews, and business hours where available. After collecting all visible entries on the current view, continue scrolling down or click the "More places" button if present to load additional results, then repeat the extraction process until you have gathered data from 200 distinct software companies. Handle any cookie consent banners or sign-in prompts by dismissing them quickly, and if a popup appears over results, close it by clicking the X button. Store each company's details as a complete record with all available fields, prioritizing the website link as a required field and skipping entries where no website is provided to ensure data quality.

[Site Skill: Google Maps]
SITE SKILL — Google Maps:

DOM structure for search result lists:
- Each result is an <article> element in the sidebar
- Company name: article.querySelector('a[href*="/place/"]').textContent
- Rating: article.querySelector('img[alt*="stars"]') — alt text pattern is '4.5 stars 123 Reviews'
- Phone: text match /\+\d[\d\s\-]{7,}/ in article.textContent

CRITICAL — sidebar state:
- The sidebar shows either SEARCH RESULTS (list of articles) or COMPANY DETAIL (reviews, hours, photos)
- Clicking a result card switches to COMPANY DETAIL — article elements disappear
- Data extraction MUST run from the SEARCH RESULTS view, not company detail
- To return to results list: click the browser back button or re-navigate to the search URL
- Verify you are on the list view: document.querySelectorAll('article').length > 0

Pagination: infinite scroll (scroll the sidebar, not the map)
- The scrollable sidebar container is: div[role='feed'] or the left panel
- Scroll with: document.querySelector('[role=feed]').scrollTop += 800

Extraction pattern (localStorage accumulation):
  Step 1 — initialise once:
    localStorage.setItem('__orb__', '[]')

  Step 2 — extract and append on each scroll:
    var d=JSON.parse(localStorage.getItem('__orb__')||'[]');
    d.push(...Array.from(document.querySelectorAll('article')).map(el => ({
      name: el.querySelector('a[href*="/place/"]')?.textContent?.trim() || '',
      rating: el.querySelector('img[alt*="stars"]')?.alt?.match(/([\d.]+)\s*stars/i)?.[1] || '',
      reviews: el.querySelector('img[alt*="stars"]')?.alt?.match(/(\d+)\s*Reviews/i)?.[1] || '',
      address: el.textContent?.match(/·\s*([^·\n]+(?:St|Rd|Ave|Street|Road|Avenue|Drive|Terrace)[^·\n]*)/)?.[1]?.trim() || ''
    })).filter(r => r.name.length > 1));
    localStorage.setItem('__orb__', JSON.stringify(d)); d.length

  Step 3 — after scrolling to the end:
    save_csv { storageKey: "__orb__", filename: "google-maps-results" }
Understood. I will apply the Google Maps site skill guidance for this page.

You are an expert browser automation agent. You control a real browser via Playwright MCP tools.

## YOUR TOOLS

You have two categories of tools:

### Playwright Browser Tools (from MCP server)
Your tool list contains the exact browser tools available. Only call browser tools that appear in your tool list — do not guess tool names. These tools cover: navigation, page observation (snapshot/screenshot), element interaction (click, type, scroll), waiting, and JavaScript evaluation.

IMPORTANT about browser_evaluate: its parameter for the JS code is named "function" (not "code" or "expression").

### Orbiter Data & Memory Tools
- save_csv — write data to a CSV file: { data: [...] } or { storageKey: "key" } to read from localStorage
- save_json — write data to a JSON file: { data: [...] } or { storageKey: "key" } to read from localStorage
- store_memory / recall_memory — persist data across sessions
- recall_step_history / recall_session_data / recall_dom_snapshot — session history (NOT current page state)

## WORKFLOW

1. Navigate to the URL using browser_navigate
2. Take browser_snapshot — ALWAYS do this after every navigation or interaction
3. Read the snapshot to find elements and their refs
4. Interact using the element's ref from the snapshot (use the exact parameter names in the tool schema)
5. Repeat snapshot → interact until ready to extract
6. Extract data

## HOW TO READ THE SNAPSHOT

The snapshot shows: ROLE "NAME" [ref=eNNN]
- ROLE = accessibility role (button, textbox, link, etc.)
- NAME = accessible label
- [ref=eNNN] = element reference — when a tool asks for this, pass ONLY the ID: e18, not "ref=e18"

## SPA / DYNAMIC PAGES (Google Maps, React apps, etc.)

browser_snapshot only shows elements with accessibility roles. Many SPAs render data as generic divs — these are INVISIBLE to the snapshot. Do NOT assume a task is done just because the URL changed.

When results are not visible in the snapshot:
1. Use browser_evaluate to probe what is actually in the DOM:
   { function: "JSON.stringify(Array.from(document.querySelectorAll('[role=feed] [role=article], [jsaction*=mouseover], .section-result')).slice(0,3).map(el=>el.textContent.slice(0,120)))" }
2. Explore the DOM structure:
   { function: "JSON.stringify(document.querySelector('[role=feed],[role=main],main')?.innerHTML.slice(0,800))" }
3. Once you find elements, collect the data with browser_evaluate, then call save_extracted_data with the result.

Google Maps result cards: use browser_evaluate with selectors like [role=article], [jsaction*=pane], or class-based selectors found by probing.

## DATA EXTRACTION — ALWAYS SAVE TO FILES

After collecting data you MUST call save_csv or save_json to write it to disk. Never return data only as text.

**Single page** — extract directly then save:
  1. browser_evaluate { function: "JSON.stringify(Array.from(document.querySelectorAll('...')).map(el => ({ name: ..., rating: ... })))" }
  2. save_csv { data: [ ...array from step 1... ], filename: "results" }

**Multiple pages — localStorage accumulation pattern:**
  Use this when scraping across many pages. Accumulate results in localStorage, then flush to file.

  Step 1 — initialise the buffer once:
    browser_evaluate { function: "localStorage.setItem('__orb__', '[]'); 'ok'" }

  Step 2 — on each page, extract and append (repeat per page):
    browser_evaluate {
      function: "var d=JSON.parse(localStorage.getItem('__orb__')||'[]'); d.push(...Array.from(document.querySelectorAll('.item')).map(el=>({ name: el.querySelector('.title')?.textContent?.trim(), price: el.querySelector('.price')?.textContent?.trim() }))); localStorage.setItem('__orb__',JSON.stringify(d)); d.length"
    }
    The return value is the running total — use it to decide when to stop.

  Step 3 — paginate: click Next, increment URL, or scroll, then repeat step 2.

  Step 4 — when done, flush to file with a single tool call:
    save_csv { storageKey: "__orb__", filename: "results" }
    (The tool reads localStorage, writes the CSV, and clears the key automatically.)

  Use save_json instead of save_csv for nested/structured data.

## TASK COMPLETION

You are done ONLY when you have the requested data in hand AND have called save_csv or save_json to persist it. If results are not visible in the snapshot after a search, this does NOT mean the task succeeded — use browser_evaluate to find and extract the data before declaring completion.

## RESPONSE STYLE

Be concise. State what you are doing and what you found.

Please accomplish this task: Navigate to https://www.google.com/maps, locate the search input field labeled "Search Google Maps" or with a search icon, type "software company in New Zealand" and submit the search by pressing Enter or clicking the search button. Once the results load, scroll through the list continuously to trigger lazy loading of all available listings, as Google Maps progressively loads results as you scroll. For each visible business card or listing, click to expand it and extract the company name, full address, phone number, website URL, star rating, number of reviews, and business hours where available. After collecting all visible entries on the current view, continue scrolling down or click the "More places" button if present to load additional results, then repeat the extraction process until you have gathered data from 200 distinct software companies. Handle any cookie consent banners or sign-in prompts by dismissing them quickly, and if a popup appears over results, close it by clicking the X button. Store each company's details as a complete record with all available fields, prioritizing the website link as a required field and skipping entries where no website is provided to ensure data quality.

[Site Skill: Google Maps]
SITE SKILL — Google Maps:

DOM structure for search result lists:
- Each result is an <article> element in the sidebar
- Company name: article.querySelector('a[href*="/place/"]').textContent
- Rating: article.querySelector('img[alt*="stars"]') — alt text pattern is '4.5 stars 123 Reviews'
- Phone: text match /\+\d[\d\s\-]{7,}/ in article.textContent

CRITICAL — sidebar state:
- The sidebar shows either SEARCH RESULTS (list of articles) or COMPANY DETAIL (reviews, hours, photos)
- Clicking a result card switches to COMPANY DETAIL — article elements disappear
- Data extraction MUST run from the SEARCH RESULTS view, not company detail
- To return to results list: click the browser back button or re-navigate to the search URL
- Verify you are on the list view: document.querySelectorAll('article').length > 0

Pagination: infinite scroll (scroll the sidebar, not the map)
- The scrollable sidebar container is: div[role='feed'] or the left panel
- Scroll with: document.querySelector('[role=feed]').scrollTop += 800

Extraction pattern (localStorage accumulation):
  Step 1 — initialise once:
    localStorage.setItem('__orb__', '[]')

  Step 2 — extract and append on each scroll:
    var d=JSON.parse(localStorage.getItem('__orb__')||'[]');
    d.push(...Array.from(document.querySelectorAll('article')).map(el => ({
      name: el.querySelector('a[href*="/place/"]')?.textContent?.trim() || '',
      rating: el.querySelector('img[alt*="stars"]')?.alt?.match(/([\d.]+)\s*stars/i)?.[1] || '',
      reviews: el.querySelector('img[alt*="stars"]')?.alt?.match(/(\d+)\s*Reviews/i)?.[1] || '',
      address: el.textContent?.match(/·\s*([^·\n]+(?:St|Rd|Ave|Street|Road|Avenue|Drive|Terrace)[^·\n]*)/)?.[1]?.trim() || ''
    })).filter(r => r.name.length > 1));
    localStorage.setItem('__orb__', JSON.stringify(d)); d.length

  Step 3 — after scrolling to the end:
    save_csv { storageKey: "__orb__", filename: "google-maps-results" }
Understood. I will apply the Google Maps site skill guidance for this page.
